_comment: Built-in ingredient concern database. Each entry uses the normalized lowercase ingredient name as key.
_schema: name, score (0-10 EWG-equivalent), concerns (array), description, aliases (array of alternate names)

formaldehyde.name: Formaldehyde
formaldehyde.description: Known human carcinogen. Used as a preservative. Released by formaldehyde-releasing preservatives.

dmdm hydantoin.name: DMDM Hydantoin
dmdm hydantoin.description: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative. Releases formaldehyde into products over time.

diazolidinyl urea.name: Diazolidinyl Urea
diazolidinyl urea.description: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative. Releases more formaldehyde than imidazolidinyl urea.

bronopol.name: Bronopol
bronopol.description: Preservative that can form carcinogenic nitrosamines.

methylparaben.name: Methylparaben
methylparaben.description: Paraben preservative with weak estrogenic activity.

propylparaben.name: Propylparaben
propylparaben.description: Paraben with significant estrogenic activity; restricted in EU for leave-on products.

butylparaben.name: Butylparaben
butylparaben.description: Paraben with the strongest estrogenic activity among common parabens.

ethylparaben.name: Ethylparaben
ethylparaben.description: Mild paraben preservative; generally considered lower risk than butyl/propyl parabens.

isobutylparaben.name: Isobutylparaben
isobutylparaben.description: Paraben with high hormonal disruption potential.

isopropylparaben.name: Isopropylparaben
isopropylparaben.description: Paraben with estrogenic activity; banned in some jurisdictions.

oxybenzone.name: Oxybenzone
oxybenzone.description: UV filter that penetrates skin and accumulates in the body; significant endocrine disruption potential. Banned in Hawaii reef-protection laws.

octinoxate.name: Octinoxate
octinoxate.description: UV filter with estrogenic activity. Detected in human blood, urine, and breast milk.

homosalate.name: Homosalate
homosalate.description: UV filter that disrupts estrogen, androgen, and progesterone. Accumulates in the body.

avobenzone.name: Avobenzone
avobenzone.description: UV-A filter; generally considered safe but can be a skin sensitizer.

diethyl phthalate.name: Diethyl Phthalate
diethyl phthalate.description: Phthalate used as a solvent/plasticizer; often hidden under 'fragrance'.

dibutyl phthalate.name: Dibutyl Phthalate
dibutyl phthalate.description: Phthalate linked to reproductive toxicity. Banned in EU cosmetics.

fragrance.name: Fragrance
fragrance.description: Catch-all term hiding potentially hundreds of undisclosed chemicals including phthalates, musks, and allergens.

triclosan.name: Triclosan
triclosan.description: Antimicrobial agent banned from soaps in the US (FDA 2017); disrupts thyroid and sex hormones.

triclocarban.name: Triclocarban
triclocarban.description: Antimicrobial banned from rinse-off soaps (FDA 2017); androgenic and estrogenic activity.

bha.name: BHA (Butylated Hydroxyanisole)
bha.description: Synthetic antioxidant preservative. Possible human carcinogen per IARC; endocrine disruption concerns.

bht.name: BHT (Butylated Hydroxytoluene)
bht.description: Synthetic antioxidant preservative. Some evidence of carcinogenicity at high doses.

petrolatum.name: Petrolatum
petrolatum.description: Petroleum-derived ingredient that may be contaminated with carcinogenic polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) if not fully refined. EU requires proof of full refinement.

mineral oil.name: Mineral Oil
mineral oil.description: Petroleum byproduct; contamination concerns with PAHs similar to petrolatum.

talc.name: Talc
talc.description: Mineral that may be contaminated with asbestos. High-risk in powder form (inhalation). Johnson & Johnson baby powder litigation involved talc contamination.

lead acetate.name: Lead Acetate
lead acetate.description: Lead compound used in hair dyes. Banned in EU, Canada, and Japan. Known neurotoxin.

mercury.name: Mercury / Thimerosal
mercury.description: Mercury compounds are neurotoxic. Banned in cosmetics in the EU, Canada, and US (except some eye products).

hydroquinone.name: Hydroquinone
hydroquinone.description: Skin lightening agent. Possible carcinogen (IARC Group 3). Banned in EU cosmetics over the counter.

coal tar.name: Coal Tar
coal tar.description: Known human carcinogen. Used in dandruff/psoriasis treatments; banned in EU cosmetics.

p-phenylenediamine.name: p-Phenylenediamine
p-phenylenediamine.description: Oxidative hair dye ingredient; potent allergen and possible carcinogen.

resorcinol.name: Resorcinol
resorcinol.description: Used in hair dyes; thyroid disruption potential. Restricted in EU.

ethanolamines.name: Ethanolamines (DEA/MEA/TEA)
ethanolamines.description: Emulsifiers and pH adjusters that can form carcinogenic nitrosamines. DEA restricted in EU.

sodium lauryl sulfate.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
sodium lauryl sulfate.description: Surfactant that can irritate and disrupt the skin barrier, especially with repeated use.

polyethylene glycol.name: Polyethylene Glycol (PEG)
polyethylene glycol.description: PEG compounds may be contaminated with carcinogenic 1,4-dioxane and ethylene oxide. Risk depends on purity.

1,4-dioxane.name: 1,4-Dioxane
1,4-dioxane.description: Carcinogenic contaminant found in products containing ethoxylated ingredients (PEGs, sodium laureth sulfate). Not directly added but present as contamination.

sodium laureth sulfate.name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
sodium laureth sulfate.description: Ethoxylated surfactant that may contain 1,4-dioxane and ethylene oxide as trace contaminants.

cocamidopropyl betaine.name: Cocamidopropyl Betaine
cocamidopropyl betaine.description: Surfactant derived from coconut oil; named allergen of the year (2004) by the American Contact Dermatitis Society.

cyclomethicone.name: Cyclomethicone (D4/D5)
cyclomethicone.description: Cyclic silicones D4 and D5 are endocrine disruptors restricted in EU rinse-off products.

toluene.name: Toluene
toluene.description: Solvent used in nail polish; neurotoxic, causes developmental harm. Banned in EU cosmetics.

formaldehyde resin.name: Formaldehyde Resin (Tosylamide)
formaldehyde resin.description: Common nail polish allergen. Distinct from formaldehyde but still sensitizing.

dihydroxyacetone.name: Dihydroxyacetone
dihydroxyacetone.description: Self-tanning active ingredient. Some concern about free radical generation; classified as possibly mutagenic.

retinyl palmitate.name: Retinyl Palmitate
retinyl palmitate.description: Vitamin A ester that may accelerate skin tumor development when used in sun-exposed products per NTP studies.

retinol.name: Retinol
retinol.description: Vitamin A derivative. High doses are teratogenic; FDA warns against use during pregnancy.

styrene.name: Styrene
styrene.description: Possible human carcinogen (IARC Group 2A). Used in some nail products.

ethylene oxide.name: Ethylene Oxide
ethylene oxide.description: Known human carcinogen (IARC Group 1). Found as a trace contaminant in ethoxylated cosmetic ingredients.

siloxane.name: Siloxane / D4
siloxane.description: Cyclic siloxanes (D4, D5) are persistent environmental contaminants and endocrine disruptors. D4 banned in EU wash-off cosmetics.

benzalkonium chloride.name: Benzalkonium Chloride
benzalkonium chloride.description: Antimicrobial preservative and surfactant; potent skin and eye irritant, common allergen.

methylisothiazolinone.name: Methylisothiazolinone
methylisothiazolinone.description: Preservative that is a potent contact allergen; banned in leave-on products in EU. Neurotoxic in lab studies.

methylchloroisothiazolinone.name: Methylchloroisothiazolinone
methylchloroisothiazolinone.description: Preservative (often combined with MI); potent allergen. Severely restricted in EU leave-on products.

iodopropynyl butylcarbamate.name: Iodopropynyl Butylcarbamate
iodopropynyl butylcarbamate.description: Preservative that may affect the thyroid; banned in EU body lotion for children under 3.

teflon.name: PTFE / Teflon
teflon.description: Used in cosmetics for texture; concern about PFAS contamination from manufacturing.

pfas.name: PFAS (Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances)
pfas.description: 'Forever chemicals' linked to cancer and endocrine disruption. Found in some cosmetics and water-resistant products.

titanium dioxide.name: Titanium Dioxide
titanium dioxide.description: Generally safe in sunscreens but classified as possible carcinogen when inhaled as nano-particles (IARC 2B). EU prohibits in spray-form cosmetics.

aluminum compounds.name: Aluminum Compounds
aluminum compounds.description: Used in antiperspirants; accumulates in breast tissue. Weak estrogenic activity in lab studies.

phenoxyethanol.name: Phenoxyethanol
phenoxyethanol.description: Preservative linked to allergic reactions and skin irritation. FDA warned against use in nipple cream (infant exposure).

chlorphenesin.name: Chlorphenesin
chlorphenesin.description: Preservative and muscle relaxant. May cause neurotoxic effects; restricted in Japan.

benzophenone.name: Benzophenone
benzophenone.description: UV stabilizer (not the sunscreen). IARC Group 2B possible carcinogen; endocrine disruption.

styrene/acrylates copolymer.name: Styrene/Acrylates Copolymer
styrene/acrylates copolymer.description: Film-forming polymer; styrene component is a possible carcinogen.

nitrosamines.name: Nitrosamines
nitrosamines.description: Carcinogenic impurities that can form from amines + nitrosating agents in cosmetics.
